explanation.title: Default Parameters, Rest Arguments, and Returning Objects

codeExample.title: Flexible Statistics Calculator
codeExample.explanation:
- `function computeStats(...values)` — rest parameter; all arguments are collected into the `values` array
- `values.reduce((sum, v) => sum + v, 0)` — iterates the array, accumulating a running sum starting from `0`
- `total` inside the returned object — shorthand property: when key and variable have the same name, write it once
- `Math.max(...values)` — the spread operator `...` expands the array into individual arguments for `Math.max`
- `function printStats(stats, label = "Data")` — `label` defaults to `"Data"` if the caller passes only one argument

exercise.title: Build a Configurable Discount Function
exercise.instructions:
Write a function applyDiscount(price, discountPct = 10) where discountPct defaults to 10. Return an object with: original (the price), discountAmount (price * discountPct / 100), and finalPrice (original minus discount). Call it twice and print all three properties.

Test: applyDiscount(2000) → discountAmount 200, finalPrice 1800.
Test: applyDiscount(2000, 25) → discountAmount 500, finalPrice 1500.
hints: 💡 Return an object literal: return { original: price, discountAmount, finalPrice };
💡 discountAmount and finalPrice are already computed — just include them in the returned object.
💡 The shorthand { discountAmount } is the same as { discountAmount: discountAmount } when key and variable share the same name.

questions.question: What does a rest parameter (...args) collect in a function?
options: All keyword arguments as an object
All remaining positional arguments into an array
questions.explanation: A rest parameter, written as ...paramName, collects all remaining positional arguments that were not matched by earlier parameters into a real JavaScript array. For example, function f(first, ...rest) called as f(1, 2, 3, 4) gives first=1 and rest=[2, 3, 4].

questions.question: Where must default parameters appear in a function signature?
options: They can appear in any position
Default parameters are not allowed in JavaScript
questions.explanation: Default parameters must come after all required (non-default) parameters. Placing a default parameter before a required one causes confusion about which argument maps to which parameter. JavaScript will throw a SyntaxError or behave unexpectedly if defaults precede required parameters.
